message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_pthread_rwlock (bd-dvr22 priority-7)

## FULL REVIEW MODE verification

cargo test --workspace --all-targets this turn: only bd-9hq64
(pthread_key_create destructor not invoked on thread exit) still
failing, already filed. No new regressions, no SIGABRTs.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

## Fuzz gap — bd-dvr22 priority-7 (pthread rwlock subset)

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_rwlock.rs covers
the pthread rwlock + rwlockattr surface (~13 symbols):

  pthread_rwlock_init / _destroy / _rdlock / _wrlock /
  _tryrdlock / _trywrlock / _timedrdlock / _timedwrlock /
  _unlock
  pthread_rwlockattr_init / _destroy / _setpshared / _getpshared

14 op variants with a per-rwlock State enum (Live / ReadHeld /
WriteHeld / Stale) plus read_depth tracking.

Oracles beyond the now-usual rc-contract and round-trip:

- **Reader-reader compat** (POSIX §2.9.5): after rdlock(rw), a
  second tryrdlock(rw) from the same thread MUST succeed.
  Violation means our impl is rejecting compatible readers,
  which would dead-lock every real reader-dominant workload.
- **Writer-writer exclusion**: after wrlock(rw), a second
  trywrlock(rw) from the same thread MUST fail (EDEADLK or
  EBUSY). Violation means two writers can coexist, breaking
  the rwlock's core guarantee.
- Stale-handle invariant: tryrdlock / trywrlock on a
  Stale-state rwlock must not succeed.
- Timeouts bounded to < 1 ms via short_abstime() so libFuzzer
  never blocks.

Single-threaded per iteration; RWLOCK global serializes;
cleanup() drains any held state before destroy.

Validation: cargo build clean (0.4s); cargo clippy --no-deps
-- -D warnings clean first-try.

bd-dvr22 priority-7 progress: mutex + cond + keys + rwlock
subsets shipped. Remaining: barrier, once + c11threads (TSan).

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -105,6 +105,11 @@ name = "fuzz_pthread_keys"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_keys.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_pthread_rwlock"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_rwlock.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resolver"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resolver.rs"
